column1: # templates/postures.tsv — the CANONICAL CONFIG POSTURES (HERD-153): the committed, authoritative
# set of named operating postures for a herd control room. A "posture" is a small, human-named bundle
# of .herd/config KEY=VALUE settings that together select one coherent governance stance (who merges,
# whether a human approves, whether a push is gated, what pipeline steps run). This file is the SINGLE
# SOURCE OF TRUTH for those bundles: the posture-matrix sim (scripts/herd/sim/sandbox-posture-matrix.sh)
# proves the gate loop under each posture here, and HERD-141 (init v2 posture profiles, shipped) CONSUMES
# this file to offer these postures at `herd init` time and render the chosen bundle into a
# project's .herd/config. Keep it authoritative: add a posture here (not in the sim or the installer)
# and every consumer picks it up.
# Format — one TAB-SEPARATED row per posture (columns separated by a literal tab, NOT spaces):
#     name <TAB> keys <TAB> intent
#   name    kebab-case posture id — stable; used as the sim/scorecard key and the HERD-141 profile id.
#   keys    the posture's config bundle: a SPACE-separated list of KEY=VALUE pairs, each an existing
#           .herd/config key (see templates/capabilities.tsv / templates/config.example). An empty or
#           absent key falls back to its engine default, so a posture lists ONLY the keys it changes.
#           The special token STEPS_PROFILE=<id> is NOT a config key: it names a pipeline-steps profile
#           (a templates/steps.example-shaped .herd/steps.tsv) that HERD-141 renders and the sim builds
#           as a fixture — it is how a posture pulls in a steps.tsv stage without inlining the TSV here.
#   intent  one-line human description of the stance — what this posture is FOR (surfaced by HERD-141).
# Blank lines and lines whose first field starts with '#' are ignored; the columns are tab-separated.
# The six rows below are the canonical postures the posture-matrix sim asserts an invariant for.
name
solo-auto
column3: Solo operator, full autonomy: the watcher runs every gate and auto-merges — today's fixture default, no human in the merge loop.

column1: team-approve
column3: Team review: gates run to green, then the merge HOLDS for a sha-keyed human approval — nothing lands without an explicit approve.

column1: gated-push
column3: Locked-down egress: a finished builder HOLDS before pushing; a human approves the push, and PRs open as drafts — nothing reaches the remote unreviewed.

column1: custom-steps
column3: Operator-defined pipeline: a .herd/steps.tsv adds an approve-stage the lane must hold on and a human must release, on top of the built-in review/health floor.

column1: observe-only
column3: Watch-only shadow mode: the watcher runs every gate and reports/notifies but NEVER merges — for dry-running the loop against a repo you do not yet let it land into.

column1: full-auto
column2: MERGE_POLICY=auto REVIEW_AUTOFIX=true HEALTHCHECK_AUTOFIX=true COORDINATOR_AUTONOMY=full DEAD_BUILDER_AUTORESPAWN=on HUMAN_VERIFY_POLICY=coordinator STALE_BASE_AUTOFIX=on SWEEP_AUTO=auto
column3: Engine-autonomous ops: the human steers WHAT — or delegates even that to a full-autonomy coordinator — while the engine owns the rest: auto-merge on green, auto-fix BLOCK reviews and red healthchecks, respawn dead builders, self-heal stale bases, run human-verify steps via the coordinator, and self-sweep debris — a hands-off unattended loop.
